evidence_rea: Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor had visited the other website.
evidence_reg: Where the icons are presented electronically they shall be machine-readable.
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: REA describes the behavior of embedded content, whereas REG requires electronic icons to be machine-readable. The actors and objects are also different in the REA and REG.

evidence_rea: Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor had visited the other website.
evidence_reg: Paragraph 1 shall not apply if one of the following applies: (e) processing relates to personal data which are manifestly made public by the data subject;
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: REA refers to behavior of embedded web content. REG defines conditions where certain data processing rules should not apply. The actors, actions and objects are unrelated.

evidence_rea: Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor had visited the other website.
evidence_reg: Paragraphs 1 to 4 shall not apply where and insofar as:
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: REA describes the behaviour of embedded web content, while REG presents conditions for where certain regulation paragraphs won't apply. They do not share similar actors, actions or objects.
